summary: Implementation correctly fixes string reverse slice `[::-1]` returning empty string (#1111). All spec requirements R6–R12 are satisfied. The fix in mb_str_slice_full (string_ops.rs lines 119–126) uses None-aware match expressions: absent start defaults to len-1 directly, absent stop defaults to literal -1, bypassing clamp_rev_str in both cases. Explicit values still go through clamp_rev_str. The spec has no ## Test Plan section, so the hard reject rule does not apply — nevertheless 14 unit tests covering all scenarios (S1–S9 plus boundary cases) are present and all pass. xfail fixture deleted and conformance test passes. Main spec updated with R6 decision table.

code_matches_spec.notes: R6 (absent stop → -1 literal): string_ops.rs lines 123–126, `match stop.as_int() { Some(v) => clamp_rev_str(v, len), None => -1 }`. R7 (absent start → len-1): lines 119–122, `match start.as_int() { Some(v) => clamp_rev_str(v, len), None => len - 1 }`. R8 (explicit negative start still clamp_rev_str): Some(v) branch used, test S4 'abcdef'[-2::-1]=>'edcba' passes. R9 (explicit stop still clamp_rev_str): Some(v) branch, test S5 'abcdef'[4:1:-1]=>'edc' passes. R10 (positive step unchanged): lines 111–114, normalize_index path untouched. R11 (Unicode): chars().collect() codepoint iteration, test S8 '你好世界'[::-1]=>'界世好你' passes. R12 (xfail fixture converted): string_edge_cases_xfail.py and .expected deleted (confirmed absent on disk); 4 reverse slice cases merged into string_edge_cases.py lines 18–22 and matching outputs appended to .expected lines 9–12. Constraints respected: clamp_rev_str function unmodified, mb_str_slice (2-arg) unaffected, do_not_touch list functions intact.

tests_present_when_test_plan_exists.notes: Spec has no ## Test Plan section (sections present: Overview, Source Files, Requirements, Acceptance Criteria, Scenarios, Changes). Hard reject rule does not apply. Implementation adds 14 #[test] functions at string_ops.rs lines 2367–2547 under #[cfg(test)] mod tests. Tests cover: S1 full reverse, S2 single-char, S3 empty string, S4 explicit negative start, S5 explicit start+stop, S6 positive step, S7 step=-2, S8 unicode, plus boundary cases: step=0, explicit start=0, explicit stop=0, forward absent start/stop, step=2, explicit negative stop.

no_regressions.notes: cargo test -p cclab-mamba --lib -- test_str_slice: 14 passed, 0 failed (2876 other tests filtered out, none failing). Conformance test data_structures/string_edge_cases.py: ok, 1 passed. Fix is localized to the negative-step absent-start/stop match arms only; the positive-step branch and all other string functions are untouched.

soft_findings.title: Test labels in source reference spec requirement IDs that differ from the spec
soft_findings.details: The doc comments on the new tests (e.g. `/// S1: Full reverse slice s[::-1] produces reversed string (R1, R2, R6)`) cite requirement IDs R1/R2/R6 which don't all match the spec's requirement numbering (spec uses R6, R7, R8, R9, R10, R11, R12). The test correctly exercises the right behaviors; the requirement citations are just minor annotation inaccuracies. Not a blocking issue.
soft_findings.recommendation: Update doc comment requirement citations from (R1, R2, R6) to (R6, R7, R11) to match the spec's actual requirement IDs.
